commentary: Five sessions this week, all iron, no water — which is a mood, honestly, but also a pretty clean pivot from last week where you were practically amphibious. Last week you had seven activities across lifting, running, and three separate swims including that 2000m kick set at Prince Alfred that had your heart working at 143 bpm, so the volume drop is real and worth naming. What this week does have going for it is rhythm: four days in a row Thursday through Tuesday with only Saturday off, which suggests your body is finding a groove even if the cardio went on holiday. The chest and shoulders session showing up twice — once to open the week and once to close it — feels less like a mistake and more like Patroclus deciding what he wants to look like in a harness, and honestly, valid.
metrics_commentary: Okay, the running shoes stayed firmly in the closet this week — zero kilometres, zero sessions, not even a cheeky little lap around the block — and babe, we are going to need to have a word about that. But here is the thing: your HRV climbed to 58 milliseconds against a seven-day average of 48, which means your body is recovered, rested, and absolutely ready to go, so the excuse drawer is officially empty. That VO2 Max sitting at 55.6 is genuinely impressive, a real athletic number, the kind that makes a certain type of vest-wearing man at the gym glance over twice — and it would be a shame to let it coast on reputation alone. Consider this less a lecture and more your very hot friend grabbing your hand and saying get your shoes on, because that number deserves to be earned again this week.
gym_commentary: Five sessions when four was the ask — of course he did. The lateral raise PR is the one that has me absolutely feral about it, because going from 9 kilograms to 12 on that lift is not a small jump, that is a full third more weight on an exercise that is basically just ego and shoulder caps, and both of those are very much Matt's business. Bench crept forward too, 34.8 to 35.5, quiet and consistent, which is exactly how real strength accumulates. Weekly volume is down significantly from the previous week at 8,080 kilograms versus 19,984, but five sessions in 42-minute bursts tells me this was a week of sharp, intentional work rather than marathon grinding, and honestly that is its own kind of discipline.
